ORDINANCE NO. 25-020
AN OrdInanCe Of THE COUnTY OF ORanGe, CAlIFOrnIA
REPEALING THE 2O21. EDITION OF THE UNIFORM
MECHANICAL CODE, REPEALING THE 2022 EDITION OF THe
CALIFORNIA MECHANICAL CODE, REPEALING ORDINANCE
22-006 AND ADOPTING BY REFERENCE THE UNIFORM
MECHANICAL CODE, 2024 EDITION, AND ThE CALIFORNIA
MECHANICAL CODE, 2025 EDITION, AmENDMENTS
THERETO.
The Board of Supervisors of the County of Orange, California, does ordain as follows:
SECTION 1. Article 1 of Division 5 of Title 7 of the Orange County Codifled Ordinances is hereby
repealed.
SECTION 2. Article 1 of Division 5 is hereby added to Ttle 7 of the Orange
County Codified Ordinances to read as follows:
CALIFORNIA MECHANICAL CODE, 2025 EDITION AND AMENDMENTS THERETO
a.
The Board of Supervisors of the County of Orange hereby adopts the Uniform Mechanical Code,
2024 Edition and the Calfornia Mechanical Code, 2025 Edition as published by the International
Association of Plumbing and Mechanical Officials (IAPMO), including Appendices A through C,
Division II of Chapter 1, except such portions as are deleted, modified, or amended in this
Article. Chapter 1 of the Uniform Mechanical Code is replaced with Division II of Chapter 1 of the
California Mechanical Code.
b.
The purpose of this Code is to prescribe regulations for the protection of the public health and
safety and to establish minimum regulations for the installation, alteration, design, construction,
quality of materials, location, operation, and maintenance of heating, ventilating, comfort
and the inspection thereof.
C.
Not less than one (1) copy of each has been made and is now filed in the office of the OC Public

Sec. 7-5-4. Section 107.3 added.

Section 107.3 NOTICE, ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING, APPEAL
Whenever the Building Official issues a Condemnation Order in accordance with the provisions of
Section 106.6 of Division II of Chapter 1 of the California Mechanical Code, or a Stop Work Order in
accordance with the provisions of Section 106.4 of said Code, the procedures for notice, administrative
hearing, and appeal may be instituted in accordance with the provisions of Section 7-1-16 - UNSAFE
BUILDING, NUISANCE, NOTICE, ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING, APPEAL of the Orange County Codified
Ordinance , The remedies and procedures here provided for shall be cumulative and in addition to the
Penalties."

Sec. 7-5-6. Section 104.4 amended.
Section 104.4 of said Division II of Chapter 1 of the California Mechanical Code is hereby
amended to read as follows:
Section 104.4.1 Issuance. The application, plans and specifications and other data filed by an
applicant for permit shall be reviewed by the Building Official. Such plans may be reviewed by other
Building Official finds that the work described in an application for a permit and the plans,
specifications and other data filed therewith conform to the requirements of this Code and other
pertinent laws and ordinances and that the fees as established by the Board of Supervisors have been
paid, he shall issue a permit therefor to the applicant.
When the Building Official issues the permit where plans are required, he shall endorse in writing
or stamp the plans and specifications "APPROVED." Such approved plans and specifications shall not be
changed, modified or altered without authorization from the Building Official, and all work reguiated by

104.4.3 Expiratlon. Every permit issued by the Building Offcial under the provisions of this Code
shall expire by limitation and become null and void if the work authorized by such permit is not
commenced within twelve months from the date of such permit, or if the work authorized by such
hundred eighty (18o) days. Before such work can be commenced or recommenced, a permit shall first
be extended or re-issued.
